primary_intent: Explain the 2026-06-05 US market selloff and persist a source-backed working view
secondary_intents: Set refresh triggers for Monday premarket
Preserve evidence boundaries before any position or portfolio review
execution_order: live data gate -> macro trigger -> index and sector concentration -> evidence log -> working-view package
research_object: US major indices and AI/semiconductor-led selloff
lens_selection_basis: The user asked why the market fell so much, requiring separation of normal noise, sharp selloff, and crash/panic labels.
lens_data_required: Index moves, rate reaction, sector concentration, and cross-asset stress checks.
lens_failure_mode: Treating a media attribution or price move as proof of fundamental deterioration.
quote_time: 2026-06-05 US market close, as reported by AP/Reuters-syndicated market coverage
source_boundary: BLS official labor release plus AP, Reuters-syndicated, and Axios market coverage; no direct exchange feed, credit-spread feed, or options-positioning feed retained.
routing_basis: The prompt began as a time-sensitive market question and was upgraded to a standard documented research package at user request.
routing_mismatch_risk: Medium if read as an actionable portfolio decision; this package explains market causality and refresh triggers only.
followup_questions: Refresh QQQ/SPY/SMH, Treasury yields, VIX, and credit spreads before 2026-06-08 US premarket?
If this affects real holdings, route next to position_review or portfolio_construction_review with weights and risk budget?
